steps.title: Recognize that we need AA similarity
steps.content: We already know that ∠A = ∠P = 52°, so the triangles share one angle pair. To prove similarity using AA (Angle-Angle), we need just one more pair of equal angles. Let's see which choice provides this information.

steps.title: Test choice C to find all angles
steps.content:
If ∠B = 34° and ∠R = 94° (from choice C), we can find the remaining angles using the fact that angles in a triangle sum to 180°.

Triangle ABC:
$$∠C = 180° - 52° - 34° = 94°$$

Triangle PQR:
$$∠Q = 180° - 52° - 94° = 34°$$

Now all three angle pairs match:
  • ∠A = ∠P = 52°
  • ∠B = ∠Q = 34°
  • ∠C = ∠R = 94°

By AA similarity, the triangles are similar. That matches choice C — C is correct.
